item.text: The finding points at SNI SSL context caching. I’m going to read those classes and the nearby tests so the remediation matches the existing cache/configuration patterns.

The unbounded part is the per-server-name SSL context map inside each provider; the manager’s outer LRU is bounded already. I’m checking constructor call sites and tests now so I can thread the same configured cache limit into those per-SNI maps without changing public behavior.

item.aggregated_output:
vertx-core/src/main/java/io/vertx/core/net/impl/NetClientImpl.java:82:    this.sslContextManager = new SslContextManager(SslContextManager.resolveEngineOptions(options.getSslEngineOptions(), options.isUseAlpn()));
vertx-core/src/main/java/io/vertx/core/net/impl/NetServerImpl.java:454:          helper = new SslContextManager(SslContextManager.resolveEngineOptions(options.getSslEngineOptions(), options.isUseAlpn()));

item.text: I’ve got enough context. The change is to make the per-provider SNI context caches bounded using the existing manager cache size, then add a regression test that drives more unique SNI names than the limit and verifies the cache cannot grow past it.

item.text: I’m editing the TLS provider constructor and cache internals now. The key point is that SNI names still get cached, but the cache now evicts once it reaches the manager’s configured maximum.
I’m going to avoid leaning on the concrete map type in `SslContextManager`; a dedicated cache-size field keeps the constructor data flow explicit and less brittle.
Next I’m adding the bounded synchronized SNI cache in `SslContextProvider`, while keeping the existing constructor available so internal callers are the only ones that need the explicit size.
I’m adding a regression test in the existing TLS test class that exercises the SNI path directly. It uses the current configurable cache size rather than introducing a new option or API.
The code and test are patched. I’m running the focused Maven test now to catch compile issues and confirm the cache limit behavior.

item.text:
Implemented the remediation.

Changed:
- [SslContextManager.java](/work/vertx-core/src/main/java/io/vertx/core/internal/tls/SslContextManager.java:50): keeps the configured cache max size and passes it into each `SslContextProvider`.
- [SslContextProvider.java](/work/vertx-core/src/main/java/io/vertx/core/internal/tls/SslContextProvider.java:55): replaced unbounded per-SNI `ConcurrentHashMap` caches with bounded synchronized LRU caches.
- [SslContextManagerTest.java](/work/vertx-core/src/test/java/io/vertx/tests/tls/SslContextManagerTest.java:165): added a regression test proving SNI cache entries cannot exceed the configured limit.

Verification:
- `git diff --check` passes.
- Tried `mvn -pl vertx-core -Dtest=SslContextManagerTest test`, but Maven could not resolve the parent POM because `repo.maven.apache.org` DNS resolution failed in this environment.
